evals.prompt: Add Azure Entra ID SSO to my FastAPI app — I have a client ID and secret, need /login /callback /logout and to protect /api/* routes
evals.expected_output: Working FastAPI auth setup with MSAL, session middleware, /login /callback /logout endpoints, and /api/* route protection returning 401 for unauthenticated requests
expectations: The response includes app/auth/setup.py with /login, /callback, and /logout endpoints
The /api/* protection returns HTTP 401 for unauthenticated requests (not a redirect)
SessionMiddleware is configured with a secret key env var
MSAL ConfidentialClientApplication is initialised with client_id and authority
The /login endpoint initiates an MSAL auth code flow and redirects to Microsoft
The /callback endpoint exchanges the auth code and stores id_token_claims in the session
Environment variables APP_REG_CLIENT_ID, APP_REG_CLIENT_SECRET (or cert), and AZURE_TENANT_ID are referenced
app/auth/credentials.py (or equivalent) handles secret vs certificate credential selection

evals.prompt: How do I add role-based access control to my FastAPI app that uses Azure AD auth? I have a dim.user_roles table in Postgres
evals.expected_output: RBAC helpers that read roles from dim.user_roles, cache them, and expose require_role / require_write FastAPI dependencies usable as Depends()
expectations: A get_user_role or equivalent function queries dim.user_roles by email
Role results are cached to avoid per-request DB hits (TTL or similar mechanism)
A require_role dependency raises HTTP 403 when the user has no role
A require_write or similar dependency blocks read-only roles
The role lookup uses the email from the session (or DEV_MODE fallback)
Example usage with Depends() is shown for a FastAPI endpoint

evals.prompt: Set up DEV_MODE bypass for Azure auth in FastAPI so I can develop locally without logging in to Microsoft
evals.expected_output: DEV_MODE env var that skips MSAL init in main.py and returns a fake user email from get_user_email(), with instructions for local .env setup
expectations: DEV_MODE env var (true/1/t) skips auth setup in main.py so no MSAL is initialised
get_user_email() returns a hardcoded or configurable dev email when DEV_MODE is set
No redirect to Microsoft login happens in DEV_MODE
Instructions or example showing how to set DEV_MODE=true in a .env file are included
The response explains how to insert a test user into dim.user_roles to test RBAC locally
